title: Silly Eyes Sheet
bio: Blinko Peepers is a tiny practice sheet spirit who collects funny eye shapes like buttons. They feel giggly, blink out of order, and hide sparkle crumbs beside their favorite expressions.

steps.title: Base Shapes
steps.instruction: On the Guides layer, draw a simple page rectangle. Add six light circles in two rows to mark where each eye pair will go.
steps.hint: Keep the circles loose and evenly spaced like a friendly sticker sheet.

steps.title: Face Placement
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, turn each circle pair into different eyes: dots, ovals, sleepy curves, stars, spirals, and tiny uneven blobs.
steps.hint: Change the eyebrow angle above each pair to create a new mood fast.

steps.title: Personality Details
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, add little labels, cheek dots, sparkles, tiny lashes, and one nervous sticky note in the corner.
steps.hint: Use only one or two details per eye pair so the sheet stays readable.

steps.title: Clean Lineart
steps.instruction: On the Lineart layer, trace the best eye shapes, eyebrows, labels, sparkles, and sticky note with clean simple lines.
steps.hint: Skip extra guide circles so each eye pair looks clear and intentional.

steps.title: Color & Details
steps.instruction: On the Color + Details layer, color the sticky note, cheeks, stars, and small accents. Add a soft shadow under the sheet.
steps.hint: Use accent colors lightly so the eyes remain the main practice focus.
